revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ame-no-Koyane]][[fr:Ama-no-Koyane-no-mikoto]][[it:Ame-no-Koyane]][[ja:天児屋命]][[mni:ꯑꯃꯦ-ꯅꯣ-ꯀꯣꯌꯅꯦ]][[pt:Ame-no-Koyane]][[ru:Амэ-но коянэ-но микото]][[simple:Ame-no-Koyane]][[zh:天兒屋命]]
{{Short description|Kami and Male deity in Shinto|qq=}} {{Infobox deity | type = Japanese | name = Ame-no-Koyane | image =; File:Amenokoyane shinbutsu-zue.jpg| caption = | god_of = God of Divine Mirror, Great Divination, Divine Affairs | script_name = Japanese | script = [[:ja:天児屋命|天児屋命]] | other_names = | cult_center = * [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Grand Shrine]] * [[Hiraoka Shrine]] * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine|en|Yoshida Shrine|qid=Q713177|lt=Yoshida Shrine|1=Yoshida Shrine}} * {{ill|Ōharano Shrine|en|Ōharano Shrine|qid=Q3594020|lt=Ōharano Shrine|1=Ōharano Shrine}} | symbols = | consort =; {{ill|Takuhata Chijihime no Mikoto|qid=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}}| parents =[[Kamimusubi]]; {{ill|Q108039939|qid=Q108039939|1=Kushimachi no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Takemikazuchi|qid=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}}| siblings = | children = * {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|ja|天押雲根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Oshikumone|qq=}}<ref>Suzuki, Matoshi. Izu Sukune Keizu Hyakkakeizuko「伊豆宿禰系図」『百家系図稿』1st book</ref> * [[Uwaharu]]<ref>Shibata, Joue. Saitama Sōsho. (1929) Volume 1: Chichibu Shi, p 111, Chichibu hikomikoto o keizu 『埼玉叢書. 第1巻』内『秩父志』内111頁「秩父彦命御系図」. Sanmeisha</ref> * [[Shitaharu]]<ref>Shibata, Joue. Saitama Sōsho. (1929) Volume 1: Chichibu Shi, p 111, Chichibu hikomikoto o keizu 『埼玉叢書. 第1巻』内『秩父志』内111頁「秩父彦命御系図」. Sanmeisha</ref> * [[Tamakushi-hime|Tamayoribime]]<ref>Nakata. Akahori, Aoki, Hori-shi: Shokeifu, 3rd book「赤堀、青木、堀氏」『諸系譜』</ref> | texts = ''[[Kojiki]]'', ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', ''Izumo-no-kuni Fudoki'' | gender = {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}} | offspring = {{ill|Ame-no-Oshikumone|qid=Q91088537|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}; {{ill|Takuhata Chijihime no Mikoto|qid=Q11110848|1=Takuhadachiji-hime}}; {{ill|Uwaharu and Shitaharu|qid=Q10940723|1=Uwaharu and Shitaharu}} | relatives = {{ill|Q106107374|qid=Q106107374|1=Namikarinene no Mikoto}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Kasuga Gongen|qid=Q11088804|1=Kasuga Gongen}}; {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|qid=Q604687|1=Kṣitigarbha}}; {{ill|Omoikane|qid=Q586088|1=Omoikane}} }} '''Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto''' ([[:ja:天児屋命|天児屋命]], 天児屋根命) is a ''[[kami]]'' and a male [[Kami|Deity]] in [[Japanese mythology]] and [[Shinto]]. He is the ancestral god of the [[Nakatomi clan]], and {{ill|Fujiwara no Kamatari|en|Fujiwara no Kamatari|qid=Q433995|lt=Fujiwara no Kamatari|1=Fujiwara no Kamatari}}, the founder of the powerful [[Fujiwara clan]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Mypaedia, Amenokoyane|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|location=Japan|oclc=38516410|qq=}}</ref> An ''[[Amatsukami]]'', 'Kami of heaven', he resides in [[Takamagahara]]. == Mythology == According to ''[[Kogo Shūi]]'' and Kashima Shrine’s genealogy, Ame-no-Koyane is the son of the {{ill|Creator deity|en|Creator deity|qid=Q635155|lt=Creator deity|1=Creator deity}} [[Kamimusubi]], one of the first three gods to come into existence.<ref name=":1" /><ref> {{Cite book |last=Tanaka |title=Kashima Daiguji Keizu, Shokeifu「鹿嶋大宮司系図」『諸系譜』 |volume=1 |location=Japan|qq=}} </ref> However, according to ''Nihon Shoki'', he is the son of [[Kogotomusubi]] (興台産霊命).<ref name=":1" /> According to ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Ame-no-Koyane was "the first in charge of divine affairs, for which reason he was made to serve by performing the [[Ukehi|Greater Divination]]." He was commanded by [[Amaterasu]] to guard the divine mirror, and was known as the "'''Imperial Aide'''" at the Imperial Palace, being in charge of divine affairs of the palace.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web|first=|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto: Kami in Classic Texts: Amenokoyane|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=37|url-status=live|access-date=2021-03-27|website=Kokugakuin University|publisher=Establishment of a National Learning Institute for the Dissemination of Research on Shinto and Japanese Culture|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060508052611/http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=37 |archive-date=2006-05-08 |qq=}}</ref> According to Japanese mythology, Ame-no-Koyane performed a ritual prayer to the sun goddess [[Amaterasu]] to call her out of the cave of [[Amano-Iwato]] and bring light back to the world, after [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], the god of storms, drove her away.<ref name=":0" /> During ''[[tenson kōrin]]'', he acted as one of the five gods descending from heaven accompanying [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]], thus becoming the ancestor of Nakatomi clan.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Ueda|first=Masaaki|title=Nihon Jinmei Daijiten, Amenokoyane|publisher=Kōdansha|year=2001|isbn=4062108003|location=Japan|oclc=50718841|qq=}}</ref> == Shrines == Ame-no-Koyane is worshipped at: * [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Grand Shrine]] * [[Hiraoka Shrine]] * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine|en|Yoshida Shrine|qid=Q713177|lt=Yoshida Shrine|1=Yoshida Shrine}} * {{ill|Ōharano Shrine|en|Ōharano Shrine|qid=Q3594020|lt=Ōharano Shrine|1=Ōharano Shrine}} * and all [[Kasuga-taisha|Kasuga Shrine]]s in Japan. ==References== {{Reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp - Encyclopedia of Shinto *[https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/ameno-koyane/ Ameno Koyane] - History of Japan Database {{Jmyth navbox long|qq=}} {{Japan-myth-stub|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub|qq=}} {{Kasuga Faith|qq=}}
[[en:Ame-no-Koyane]]
